title: ADVENT: a published Goldilocks design
text: effectiveness endpoint, success code means: Pr⁡(pPFA failure−pthermal failure<0.15∣data)>0.956. \Pr(p_{\text{PFA failure}} - p_{\text{thermal failure}} < 0.15 \mid \text{data}) > 0.956. matches reported 15 percentage point absolute noninferiority margin treatment success, translating success failure. lower failure probability better, use alternative = "less". ADVENT used 1:1 randomization. design paper also reports site stratification randomly varying permuted block sizes. package can generate blocked 1:1 randomization, model site-level stratification. block = 2 rand_ratio = c(1, 1) represent 1:1 allocation. paper reports follow-12 months. ClinicalTrials.gov record reports 305 randomized subjects starting PFA arm 302 thermal arm, 12 completing arm. use prop_loss = 0.04 rough vignette-level approximation incomplete follow-. reported enrollment period implies rapid accrual, roughly 40 randomized subjects per month. use lambda = 40 make simulated calendar-time information broadly comparable. Accrual speed worth sensitivity checking: fast accrual can reduce amount observed endpoint information available interim looks. important columns : N_enrolled: selected sample size simulated trial. stop_expected_success: whether enrollment stopped current sample size appeared adequate. stop_futility: whether enrollment stopped success looked unlikely even maximum sample size. est_final: posterior mean ($p_{} - p_{}$). post_prob_ha: final posterior probability binary endpoint effect noninferiority margin.

title: Package architecture
text: goldilocks package implements Goldilocks adaptive trial design described Broglio et al. (2014). vignette provides visual overview package functions interconnected.

title: Package architecture
text: diagram shows call graph top-level simulation function (sim_trials()) core engine (survival_adapt()) internal analysis pipeline. Exported functions shown blue. Internal functions shown grey.

title: Package architecture
text: functions fall three layers:

title: Package architecture
text: sim_comp_data(): Generates complete trial dataset calling enrollment(), randomization(), pwe_sim(). survival_adapt(): Simulates single adaptive trial. Generates data via sim_comp_data(), conducts interim analyses using posterior() test_stop_success(), performs final analysis via test_final(). return_trace = TRUE, also retains compact audit trail completed interim look. sim_trials(): Top-level entry point. Runs survival_adapt() across multiple trials (optionally parallel) collates results.

title: Package architecture
text: summarise_sims(): Summarizes output sim_trials(), computing operating characteristics power, expected sample size, stopping probabilities. summarise_trial_trace(): Condenses optional single-trial interim trace one-row stopping-path summary. plot_trial_trace(): Visualizes predictive probabilities, thresholds, enrollment, observed events optional single-trial trace. plot_sim_stopping(): Visualizes stopping outcomes enrolled sample sizes across simulated trials.

title: Package architecture
text: posterior(): Estimates posterior distribution piecewise exponential hazard rates using conjugate Gamma model. analyse_data(): Applies chosen analysis method (logrank, cox, bayes-surv, bayes-bin, chisq) (imputed) dataset. impute_data(): Imputes missing event times censored subjects using pwe_impute() pwe_sim(). haz_to_prop(): Converts posterior hazard rate draws cumulative incidence proportions via ppwe().

title: Bayesian piecewise-exponential designs
text: Survival many settings – post-surgical mortality, transplant outcomes, oncology early treatment toxicity – shows higher hazard early lower steady-state hazard later. single exponential rate capture regimes; using average rate -states early survival -states late survival. piecewise-exponential model single internal cut-point often good compromise, fitting one hazard early interval another everything afterwards. package handles piecewise hazards two related arguments: cutpoints: times hazard changes. first element must 0 represents start first interval; subsequent elements internal cut-points. J intervals J elements cutpoints J hazard pieces treatment group. (cutpoints = c(0, 6) produces two intervals, [0, 6) [6, \infty), contrast default cutpoints = 0 used “Two-arm randomized trials” vignette, produces single interval non-piecewise exponential.) hazard_treatment hazard_control: vectors length J giving constant hazard interval. helper prop_to_haz() maps cumulative event probabilities given times corresponding piecewise hazards.

title: Bayesian piecewise-exponential designs
text: Suppose primary endpoint overall survival 24 months. Based prior data, expect control arm : 30% mortality 6 months (“early high-risk” window), 50% mortality 24 months overall. treatment hypothesised attenuate early-period hazard, leaving late-period hazard unchanged. Concretely, target: 18% mortality 6 months treatment arm, 40% mortality 24 months overall. set cut-point 6 months translate proportions piecewise hazards: first column hazard [0, 6) months second column hazard 6 months onward. arms higher hazard early window. can sanity-check implied survival probabilities 24 months match specified running cumulative incidence computation back ppwe(): 0.50 0.40 respectively (modulo rounding).

title: Bayesian piecewise-exponential designs
text: Two practical considerations worth flagging: Empty intervals interim looks. Early interim looks may subjects follow-reaching later piecewise intervals. empty_interval argument controls intervals handled. default, empty_interval = "propagate", preserves historical package behavior propagating exposure time event counts nearest non-empty interval within treatment group, emits warning . supplies placeholder Gamma posterior interval information; fallback interim look yet generated follow-later intervals, substantive estimate intervals’ hazards. Use empty_interval = "prior" leave intervals zero exposure zero events, making posteriors prior-driven, empty_interval = "error" stop analysis whenever empty interval encountered. final analysis, intervals typically populated. Number cut-points. additional cut-point adds two hazard parameters estimate two-arm design (one per treatment group). limited interim data can make individual interval posteriors diffuse. experience, one two well-motivated cut-points (e.g., tied clinical milestone) usually sufficient; finer partitions tend add variance without commensurate bias reduction.

title: Bayesian binary outcome designs
text: posterior probability can calculated three ways: bin_method = "mc" draws beta posterior directly. Use N_mcmc control number Monte Carlo draws. bin_method = "normal" uses normal approximation posterior mean treatment-control difference. bin_method = "quadrature" uses numerical integration two-arm posterior difference, closed-form beta CDF single-arm designs. Monte Carlo method simulation error, controlled N_mcmc. Quadrature deterministic (numerical integration two-arm design) useful high-accuracy default computationally feasible. normal approximation fastest representative two-arm benchmark, used care sample sizes small event probabilities near 0 1.

Scenario 1 is the alternative (treatment OS 50%); scenario 2 is the null (treatment OS 30%)." ) out_power2 <- update(out_power, prob_ha = 0.96) out_t1error2 <- update(out_power2, hazard_treatment = hc, seed = 125) knitr::kable(   summarise_sims(list(out_power2$sims, out_t1error2$sims)),   digits = 3,   caption = "Operating characteristics with the more stringent P < 0.04 threshold (`prob_ha = 0.96`)." )

title: goldilocks 0.5.0.9000
text: Bayesian survival Monte Carlo beta-binomial analyses now use shared N_mcmc argument posterior sampling; separate bin_N argument removed. internal analysis results return posterior mean effect instead posterior draws. survival_adapt() can now return optional, tidy interim decision trace predictive probabilities, thresholds, stopping decisions, arm-level counts, relevant warnings. New helpers summarize plot individual traces simulation stopping outcomes (#57). plot_sim_stopping() now stacks stopping outcomes final enrolled sample size labels bar marginal percentage simulated trials. sim_trials() now supports reproducible PSOCK parallel execution Windows explicit backend argument. default Unix fork path retained; backend = "auto" selects appropriate implementation platform. Seeded simulations now also restore caller’s RNG state (#40). Consolidated Bayesian binomial analysis implementation posterior calculations method-specific branches co-located bayes_binomial_test(). Renamed piecewise-exponential Bayesian survival analysis method method = "bayes" method = "bayes-surv" distinguish method = "bayes-bin". Added method = "bayes-bin" Bayesian beta-binomial analysis complete binary outcomes, Monte Carlo, normal approximation, quadrature options treatment-control differences. Cox model analyses now use lower-level survival fit repeated Wald tests, avoiding formula summary overhead simulation hot paths. survival_adapt() now uses h0 null log hazard ratio Cox model tests, allowing non-inferiority testing h0 = log(margin). Added maintainer performance benchmarks simulation hot paths, including posterior probability conversion, posterior sampling, imputation, representative survival_adapt() runs (#42). Harmonized treatment-assignment terminology internal simulation helpers documentation: data use treatment = 1 treatment treatment = 0 control, posterior/imputation array indexing now described separately hazard slices (#35). sim_trials() now accepts seed argument creates independent per-trial "L'Ecuyer-CMRG" random-number streams reproducible simulations, including using multiple cores (#41). ppwe() now computes piecewise-exponential cumulative event probabilities directly cumulative hazard, avoiding row-wise calls PWEALL::pwe() Bayesian posterior summaries (#34). posterior() now computes piecewise-exponential sufficient statistics directly, avoiding survSplit() grouped dplyr summarization simulation hot path. enrollment() randomization() longer grow vectors repeatedly inside simulation loops (#44). sim_trials() summarise_sims() now use dplyr::bind_rows() faster binding simulation result data frames (#43).

title: goldilocks 0.5.0.9000
text: Piecewise Bayesian analyses now retain posterior-draw dimension N_mcmc = 1, avoiding matrix-validation error probability conversion. sim_trials() now uses default alternative hypothesis futility threshold survival_adapt(), omitted arguments define adaptive design (#47). survival_adapt() now requires interim looks strictly increasing, preventing non-chronological duplicated analyses (#48). Piecewise hazard inputs now validated finite non-negative across simulation, imputation, probability helpers. finite maxtime now required final hazard zero (#49). h0 must now single finite value, probability-scale bounds enforced Bayesian analyses (#50). Piecewise model inputs now require finite, strictly increasing cutpoints beginning zero finite study endpoint final cutpoint. validation shared simulation probability helpers (#52). enrollment() now validates complete schedule generating data, including integer sample size, finite positive rates, finite strictly increasing knots (#53). Chi-square final analyses now require imputed_final = FALSE, rather averaging chi-square results across multiple imputed final datasets without supported frequentist pooling rule (#51). sim_trials() now defaults serial execution ncores = 1L, matching documentation avoiding unexpected use available cores (#54). Chi-square analyses now error censored subjects followed end_of_study imputed analysis. survival_adapt() now works documented default success futility thresholds interim_look = NULL; thresholds ignored interim looks. survival_adapt(), sim_comp_data(), sim_trials() now validate probability, prior, positive-integer count arguments front, avoiding invalid simulations low-level downstream errors. sim_trials() now falls back ncores = 1L available core count detected. Log-rank Cox analyses now fail clear non-estimable-analysis errors test statistic computed reliably, rather allowing NA NaN values break adaptive decision logic. randomization() now requires two-element positive integer allocation vector, preventing invalid two-arm allocation schedules c(0, 1). prop_to_haz() now rejects invalid cumulative event probabilities, including non-finite values, values outside [0, 1), values decrease time. pwe_impute() now errors maxtime earlier observed conditioning time, preventing imputed records moving backward time. survival_adapt() longer dereferences disabled futility result Fn = 0, futility counters remain inert futility monitoring turned (#33). survival_adapt() sim_trials() now expose empty_interval policy Bayesian piecewise-exponential posterior calculations, allowing empty intervals use legacy propagation, prior-updating, strict errors.

title: goldilocks 0.5.0.9000
text: Modernized roxygen2 source comments use markdown tables, links, code spans, emphasis place older Rd markup appropriate. Added new vignette, “Technical details Goldilocks design”, documenting design notation, piecewise-exponential event-time model, Gamma posterior updating, posterior predictive probabilities, interim decision rules, final analysis options, simulation-based calibration. Added new vignette, “Bayesian binary outcome designs”, documenting method = "bayes-bin" two-arm single-arm complete binary endpoint analyses. Added new vignette, “ADVENT: published Goldilocks design”, showing published ADVENT pulsed field ablation trial maps method = "bayes-bin" beta-binomial non-inferiority endpoints, adaptive sample-size thresholds, cached simulation examples. Renamed vignettes consistency: “Two-arm randomized trials”, “Bayesian piecewise-exponential designs”, “Single-arm designs performance goal”, “Package architecture”. Clarified goldilocks treats enrollment time randomization time time point time--event simulations. Clarified single-arm external benchmark h0 often referred performance goal (PG) objective performance criterion (OPC). Expanded technical documentation Goldilocks predictive-probability algorithm, including notation final analysis quantities, operating characteristics, method-specific decision rules. Clarified Fn documentation state Fn = 0 disables futility monitoring. Added pkgdown light switch documentation site supports light, dark, automatic color modes. Added CRAN checks, CRAN monthly downloads, GPL-3 license badges README, reordered badge block.

title: goldilocks 0.5.0
text: survival_adapt() now supports one-sided tests (alternative = "greater" "less") method = "cox" method = "logrank". chi-square test remains two-sided (#20). method = "chisq" imputed_final = FALSE, subjects lost follow-now excluded final analysis. Previously, LTFU subjects counted non-events, diluted event rate biased chi-square test (#22). Removed unused C++ global variables dead threading code inherited deprecated fastlogranktest package. Replaced uses magrittr pipe (%>%) base pipe (|>) summarise_sims() posterior(), removed dplyr::%>% re-export NAMESPACE. posterior() now warns piecewise interval zero subjects data propagated adjacent interval. survival_adapt() now validates interim_look two-arm design least block size, interim look enrol single treatment arm rejected input error rather producing undefined interim posterior.

title: goldilocks 0.5.0
text: posterior() now propagates data zero-exposure piecewise intervals within treatment arm. Previously, propagation walked flat row order per-arm summary, empty leading interval treatment arm (e.g. look treatment arm subjects) copy control arm’s data treatment posterior, contaminating estimate across arms. posterior() also now checks front expected treatment arms present supplied data, erroring informative message instead silently returning -NA posterior slice absent arm (occur small interim look one arm enrolled subjects yet). survival_adapt() longer errors called without interim looks (interim_look = NULL). final analysis previously relied undefined loop index variable, replaced stage_trial_stopped. impute_data() longer uses hard-coded positional column subsetting ([, 1:10]). Temporary columns now dropped name, making function robust upstream changes data frame structure (#26). randomization() longer produces NA next_block loop exhausts elements multi-element block vector. index now wraps around cyclically (#31). analyse_data() now uses explicit row/column indexing extracting Cox model results, preventing silent errors summary matrix structure changes (#29). analyse_data() now imports stats::pnorm, used compute one-sided p-values Cox log-rank tests. Previously relied stats attached. Removed dead loss_to_fu <- NA assignment survival_adapt() shadowed loss_to_fu column inside within() never used. enrollment() now correctly selects enrollment rate piecewise changepoints. Previously, rate exact changepoint boundaries use rate prior interval (#28). survival_adapt() longer adds systematic perturbation (sd(time) / 1e4) survival times interim looks. Instead, boundary subject zero follow-time clamped .Machine$double.eps satisfy survSplit() requirements (#24).

title: goldilocks 0.5.0
text: Removed est_interim element survival_adapt() return-value documentation. field documented never computed returned. Documented two-stage posterior procedure used method = "bayes-surv" imputation, clarifying imputation model’s posterior influences analysis posterior (#27). Clarified prop_loss parameter documentation, explaining LTFU times drawn Uniform(0, t) event yet occurred dropout time (#25). Documented minimum interim_look requirement (least block size two-arm designs) survival_adapt() interim_look parameter. Improved “Example: Two-armed RCT” vignette: summarise_sims() operating characteristics now rendered captioned tables, section documents one-sided tests (including method = "bayes-surv" requires one-sided alternative measures effect cumulative-failure-probability scale p_treatment - p_control h0), cutpoint argument name corrected cutpoints. Added new vignette, “Bayesian decisions piecewise-exponential hazards”, demonstrating method = "bayes-surv" piecewise hazard via cutpoints prop_to_haz(), Gamma-prior / posterior decision rule cumulative-failure-probability scale, worked single-trial example. Added new vignette, “Single-arm trials”, documenting hazard_control = NULL mode (Bayesian-), role h0 benchmark failure rate, success rule Pr(p_treatment < h0) > prob_ha, worked single-trial example operating-characteristics templates.

title: goldilocks 0.5.0
text: Added unit tests enrollment(), randomization(), pwe_sim(), pwe_impute(), ppwe(), prop_to_haz(), sim_comp_data(), summarise_sims(), analyse_data(), posterior(). Added package architecture vignette function dependency diagram. Added _pkgdown.yml configuration documentation website. Added GitHub Actions workflow pkgdown site deployment. Updated GitHub Actions (actions/checkout, actions/upload-artifact) v4 v5 Node.js 24 compatibility. Added .positai, _pkgdown.yml, docs .Rbuildignore suppress R CMD check NOTEs. Removed unused appveyor.yml CI configuration file stale .Rbuildignore entry. Added docs/ .gitignore, since pkgdown site built deployed gh-pages branch GitHub Actions rather committed main branch. Added aria-label pkgdown navbar GitHub icon alt-text README hex logo downloads badge address pkgdown accessibility warnings. Updated README clarify C++ log-rank code ported now-deprecated fastlogranktest package. Clarified prior parameter documentation explicitly state Gamma rate parameterization prior shared across piecewise intervals treatment arms.
